title: Buying a Birthday Gift
summary: A woman visits a gift shop to buy a birthday present for her friend. She asks the shopkeeper for help and chooses a suitable gift.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Hi! I need to buy a birthday gift for my friend.

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Of course! How old is your friend?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: She likes reading and she loves flowers.

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: We have some nice books and also beautiful flower candles.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Oh, a flower candle sounds nice! How much is it?

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: This one is fifteen dollars. It smells like roses.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: That is perfect. Does it come in a box?

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Yes, it comes in a gift box. We can also add a ribbon.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Yes, please add a ribbon. Can I also get a birthday card?

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Sure! Birthday cards are two dollars each.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Great. I will take the candle and one card.

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: That is seventeen dollars in total.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Here you are. Thank you for your help!

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: You are welcome! I hope your friend enjoys her gift.

annotations.exampleEn: She bought a birthday gift for her sister.

annotations.meaningVi: sắp tròn hai mươi lăm tuổi
annotations.exampleEn: He is turning thirty next week.
She lit a candle on the dinner table.

annotations.meaningVi: nghe có vẻ hay, nghe thú vị
annotations.exampleEn: A picnic in the park sounds nice!
This soap smells like roses — it is lovely.
The chocolate came in a beautiful gift box.
She tied a red ribbon around the gift box.
Don't forget to write a message in the birthday card.
The shopping bag and the book cost twelve dollars in total.

annotations.meaningVi: Đây ạ (khi đưa vật gì cho ai)
annotations.exampleEn: She handed over the money and said, 'Here you are.'

annotations.meaningVi: Không có gì / Không có chi

grammarNotes.title: Does it / Does she – Questions in the present simple
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để đặt câu hỏi với ngôi thứ ba số ít (he, she, it) ở thì hiện tại đơn, dùng 'does' ở đầu câu: 'Does it come in a box?' / 'Does she like flowers?' Lưu ý: động từ sau 'does' luôn ở dạng nguyên thể (không thêm '-s'). Sai: 'Does it comes...?' Đúng: 'Does it come...?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To form a yes/no question with a third-person singular subject (he, she, it) in the present simple, put 'does' at the start: 'Does it come in a box?' Note: the verb after 'does' is always in the base form (no '-s'). Wrong: 'Does it comes...?' Correct: 'Does it come...?'

grammarNotes.title: Can I / Can you – Making requests
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'Can I...?' để xin phép làm gì hoặc xin ai điều gì: 'Can I also get a birthday card?' Dùng 'Can you...?' để yêu cầu người khác làm gì. Đây là cách đặt câu đơn giản và phổ biến nhất cho người mới học. Lịch sự hơn có thể dùng 'Could I...?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Can I...?' to ask for permission or to request something: 'Can I also get a birthday card?' Use 'Can you...?' to ask someone to do something. This is the simplest and most common way to make requests in English. For a more polite option, use 'Could I...?'

translationVi:
Người phụ nữ: Xin chào! Tôi muốn mua quà sinh nhật cho bạn tôi.
Người bán: Tất nhiên rồi! Bạn của bạn bao nhiêu tuổi?
Người phụ nữ: Cô ấy sắp tròn hai mươi lăm tuổi.
Người bán: Cô ấy thích gì?
Người phụ nữ: Cô ấy thích đọc sách và rất yêu hoa.
Người bán: Chúng tôi có một số sách hay và cả những ngọn nến hoa đẹp nữa.
Người phụ nữ: Ồ, nến hoa nghe hay đấy! Giá bao nhiêu?
Người bán: Cái này mười lăm đô. Nó có mùi hoa hồng.
Người phụ nữ: Thật tuyệt. Nó có đi kèm hộp không?
Người bán: Có, nó đi kèm hộp quà. Chúng tôi cũng có thể thêm dải ruy-băng.
Người phụ nữ: Vâng, xin thêm dải ruy-băng. Tôi có thể lấy thêm một tấm thiệp sinh nhật không?
Người bán: Được chứ! Thiệp sinh nhật giá hai đô mỗi cái.
Người phụ nữ: Tuyệt vời. Tôi sẽ lấy cây nến và một tấm thiệp.
Người bán: Tổng cộng là mười bảy đô.
Người phụ nữ: Đây ạ. Cảm ơn bạn đã giúp đỡ!
Người bán: Không có gì! Tôi hy vọng bạn của bạn thích món quà.

questions.prompt: Why is the woman at the shop?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'I need to buy a birthday gift for my friend.'
questions.hint: What does the woman say she needs to buy?

questions.prompt: How old is the woman's friend turning?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'She is turning twenty-five.'
questions.hint: What age does the woman mention?

questions.prompt: What two things does the friend like?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'She likes reading and she loves flowers.'
questions.hint: The woman mentions two hobbies or interests her friend has.

questions.prompt: What two items does the shopkeeper suggest?
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper says 'We have some nice books and also beautiful flower candles.'
questions.hint: What does the shopkeeper offer as suggestions?

questions.prompt: How much does the candle cost?
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper says 'This one is fifteen dollars.'
questions.hint: What price does the shopkeeper say for the candle?

questions.prompt: What does the candle smell like?
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper says 'It smells like roses.'
questions.hint: What flower scent does the shopkeeper mention?

questions.prompt: What does the candle come with?
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper says 'it comes in a gift box.'
questions.hint: What does the shopkeeper say is included with the candle?

questions.prompt: What extra decoration does the woman ask for?
questions.explanation: The woman says 'Yes, please add a ribbon.'
questions.hint: What decoration does the woman want added to the gift?

questions.prompt: How much does a birthday card cost?
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper says 'Birthday cards are two dollars each.'
questions.hint: What price does the shopkeeper quote for cards?

questions.prompt: What is the total cost of the woman's purchase?
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper says 'That is seventeen dollars in total.'
questions.hint: What is the candle plus the card?

questions.prompt: What does the shopkeeper say at the end?
options: I hope your friend enjoys her gift.
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper says 'I hope your friend enjoys her gift.'
questions.hint: What kind wish does the shopkeeper express?

cloze.template: Woman: Hi! I need to buy a birthday {{b1}} for my friend. Shopkeeper: Of course! How old is your friend? Woman: She is turning {{b2}}. Shopkeeper: What does she like? Woman: She likes reading and she loves {{b3}}. Shopkeeper: We have some nice books and also beautiful flower candles. Woman: Oh, a flower candle sounds nice! How much is it? Shopkeeper: This one is fifteen {{b4}}. It smells like roses. Woman: That is perfect. Does it come in a box? Shopkeeper: Yes, it comes in a gift box. We can also add a {{b5}}. Woman: Yes, please add a ribbon. Can I also get a birthday {{b6}}? Shopkeeper: Sure! Birthday cards are two dollars each. Woman: Great. I will take the candle and one card. Shopkeeper: That is {{b7}} dollars in total. Woman: Here you are. Thank you for your {{b8}}! Shopkeeper: You are welcome! I hope your friend {{b9}} her {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: 'Birthday gift' — the reason the woman is in the shop.
'Turning twenty-five' — the age the friend will be on her birthday.
'She loves flowers' — one of the two interests the woman mentions.
'Fifteen dollars' — the price of the candle.
'Add a ribbon' — a decorative touch for the gift box.
'Birthday card' — a greeting card for birthdays.
'Seventeen dollars' — fifteen for the candle plus two for the card.
'Thank you for your help' — a polite way to thank someone who assisted you.
'Enjoys her gift' — the shopkeeper wishes the friend happiness with her present.
'Her gift' refers to the candle the woman has just bought.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The woman chooses a gift based on what her friend likes. Why do you think it is important to think about the other person when choosing a gift? Is an expensive gift always better than a thoughtful one?
